message:
test(proptest): add 26 property tests for chaos_scale_harness (ft-3681t.7.4)

- proptest_chaos_scale_harness.rs: serde roundtrips for FailureClass/SloMetric/
  ScaleProfile/SloDefinition, scale profile factory invariants, harness run
  invariants (counter sums, pass/fail consistency), failure injection effects
  (context exhaustion, rch loss, connector offline), SLO evaluation logic,
  report serialization
- Close beads ft-3681t.7.4 and ft-3681t.1.5 (traceability proptest already committed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2758,7 +2758,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.2","title":"[analysis] Exhaustive flywheel_connectors capability/security inventory","description":"Extract architectural and operational capabilities from /dp/flywheel_connectors: host runtime, protocol model, registry/signature flow, sandbox zones, capability governance, mesh federation, connector lifecycle, and audit chain behavior. Capture constraints required to integrate these safely inside FrankenTerm.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inventory covers runtime, packaging, security, mesh, and audit concerns. 2. Integration constraints are explicit (trust boundaries, secret handling, revocation, failure modes). 3. Output is consumable by implementation epics without returning to source docs.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkOtter","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:26.901036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T21:31:23.233290Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T21:31:23.233290Z","close_reason":"Completed: Flywheel connectors inventory written to docs/flywheel-connectors-inventory-ft-3681t.1.2.md. Host runtime, protocol model, registry/signature flow, sandbox zones, capability governance, mesh federation, connector lifecycle, audit chain, key data types all documented with FrankenTerm integration constraints.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","architecture","fcp-integration","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:11:26.901036Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1727,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Kickoff status: initial source reconnaissance done. Next step is to formalize connector capability/security matrix (runtime lifecycle, registry/signing, sandbox zones, mesh transport, audit chain, credential model) and map each capability to FrankenTerm-native integration points.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:19:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.3","title":"[design] FrankenTerm convergence architecture spec (native mux + connector fabric)","description":"Author the definitive target architecture for FrankenTerm as the unified swarm terminal/control plane: native mux substrate, orchestration kernel, robot APIs, connector fabric integration, policy/governance plane, and observability model. This is the north-star contract for all downstream implementation beads.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Architecture document defines components, interfaces, data flows, and ownership boundaries. 2. Counts: A-CLI-001=5, A-OBS-001=11, A-OBS-002=7, A-PAT-001=19, A-EVT-001=9, A-WF-001=6, A-POL-001=2, A-POL-002=2, A-STO-001=68, A-STO-002=2, A-MCP-001=17, A-MCP-002=3. This establishes a machine-checkable baseline for ft-3681t.1.5 traceability verification.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:02:15Z"},{"id":1789,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5","author":"PinkWaterfall","text":"E2E evidence update (PinkWaterfall, 2026-03-03): executed tests/e2e/test_ft_3681t_1_5_1_traceability_matrix.sh. Scenario 1 (matrix structure) passed all checks. During Scenario 2, rch selected worker vmi1152480 and completed sync, but remote execution failed with 'Failed to connect to ubuntu@109.205.181.92, running locally'; local fallback then blocked on 'Blocking waiting for file lock on artifact directory'. I terminated the hung run to avoid indefinite stall. Artifacts: tests/e2e/logs/traceability_matrix_20260303_160341.jsonl and tests/e2e/logs/traceability_matrix_20260303_160341.stdout.log (contains rch logs and failure/fallback details).","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:07:04Z"},{"id":1791,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5","author":"PinkWaterfall","text":"Validation rerun (PinkWaterfall, 2026-03-03): reran targeted command with isolated target dir: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/.cargo-pinkwaterfall-trace/target CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test ntm_fcp_traceability_matrix --no-default-features -- --nocapture. rch selected vmi1153651 and synced, then remote execution failed and fell back local; compile completed to deterministic failure. Blocking errors are pre-existing in crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_scheduler.rs (no method queue.config at lines 550/935 and Rust 2024 pattern errors at lines 810/817). Outcome: cannot complete traceability matrix test until scheduler compile failures are cleared by owning slice. Command + failure output captured in current session transcript.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:13:41Z"},{"id":1793,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5","author":"PinkWaterfall","text":"Fresh-eyes review/fix pass (PinkWaterfall, 2026-03-03): reviewed Section 10 in docs/ft-3681t-convergence-architecture.md and fixed clarity issues. Re-validated .beads/issues.jsonl integrity with jq parse and br ready parse.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:18:54Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.5.1","title":"[traceability] Refresh implementation-anchor matrix and verification scaffold","description":"Update convergence traceability artifacts with current implementation anchors (CLI entrypoints, watcher/runtime loops, robot envelope/schema surfaces, storage/event/policy/workflow paths). Add a machine-checkable baseline matrix and lightweight verification checks/scripts that can be extended when ft-3681t.1.1 and ft-3681t.1.2 finalize.","notes":"Worker vmi1152480 was provisioned with Cairo/X11/Wayland dev packages; remote environment blocker resolved (cairo.pc/x11-xcb.pc now present). Remaining workspace-gate blockers are pre-existing repository issues outside this bead: cargo check --workspace --all-targets fails in frankenterm-gui tests (unresolved k9/benchmarking); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings fails in frankenterm/blob-leases with existing clippy-denied findings; cargo fmt --check reports extensive pre-existing formatting drift across many files.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonGlacier","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:42:52.829733Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T00:40:11.367980Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","blocked-upstream","quality-gate","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T01:42:52.829733Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2173,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.5.1","author":"GreenHorse","text":"Reserved and updated the traceability lane surfaces: docs/design/ntm-fcp-traceability-matrix.json, crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ntm_fcp_traceability_matrix.rs, tests/e2e/test_ft_3681t_1_5_1_traceability_matrix.sh, docs/design/ntm-fcp-convergence-architecture.md, and docs/ft-3681t-convergence-architecture.md. Added required_capability_ids coverage, stronger non-prose anchor validation, and remote-only RCH preflight/fail-closed incomplete reporting. Validation completed for jq/bash/rustfmt/diff hygiene; cargo validation is currently blocked because rch workers probe reports all configured workers connection_failed. Latest harness artifact: tests/e2e/logs/traceability_matrix_20260311_203915.report.incomplete.json.","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:40:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.1.6","title":"[analysis] Code-grounded current-state architecture map for ft runtime/control plane","description":"Produce a code-grounded architecture map of the current ft system (CLI, watcher runtime, robot mode, core pipelines, storage/events/workflows/policy seams) using direct references to live modules and command paths. Update existing architecture docs in-place so implementation agents have an accurate reference for ft-3681t follow-on work.\n\nDeliverables:\n1. Update docs/architecture.md with concrete module mapping and control-flow summary from crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs + frankenterm-core runtime stack.\n2. Document watcher lifecycle wiring (runtime, event bus, workflow runner, notifications, IPC, snapshot engine) and robot mode response envelope/command mapping.\n3. Add explicit notes for current backend boundary (WezTerm compatibility bridge) and where feature-gated surfaces are wired.\n4. Keep changes additive and non-disruptive to existing convergence docs.","notes":"Completed code-grounded architecture mapping update in docs/architecture.md. Added concrete workspace/module map, CLI dispatch + RobotResponse envelope notes, watcher lifecycle wiring (storage/pattern/event/workflow/runtime/services), runtime task model, core data-plane module seams, storage schema contract, robot-policy seam, and feature-gated boundary summary. Scope intentionally limited to docs/architecture.md to avoid overlap with active code slices.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyDog","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:29:06.491426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:31:17.253162Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T21:31:17.253142Z","close_reason":"Completed docs/architecture.md code-grounded architecture map update","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","architecture","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.1.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:29:06.491426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Replace FrankenRedis sibling path deps with pinned git deps for deterministic rch workers","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Replace the optional FrankenRedis dependencies in crates/frankenterm-core from sibling path references (../../../frankenredis/...) to pinned git dependencies so remote rch workers do not require an out-of-repo frankenredis checkout. Preserve local development convenience via config.toml.example override guidance, update Cargo.lock, and validate the previously worker-dependent remote cargo test path through rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:54:20.378040Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13.376532Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13.374930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:54:20.378040Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2125,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the bounded manifest/root-cause slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional FrankenRedis sibling path deps (`../../../frankenredis/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenredis` at `rev 7b00450d924e8048073745736356ae0e45a592af`.
- `Cargo.lock`: path packages for `fr-config`, `fr-expire`, `fr-persist`, `fr-protocol`, and `fr-store` now resolve to that git source.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: added a local override stanza so developers with a sibling checkout can still patch the same crates back to local paths intentionally.

Why:
- WhiteFox's prior worker failure (`failed to read .../frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`) was caused by manifest-level path assumptions inside this repo rather than by the worker's Cargo/test invocation itself.
- After the manifest change, two different `rch` workers resolved FrankenRedis through git instead of requiring an out-of-repo sibling checkout.

Validation (all offloaded through `rch` where compilation/test work was involved):
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-test cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch -- --nocapture` -> remote success on `vmi1152480`; test `build_coord::tests::command_uses_rch_detects_common_prefix_shapes` passed.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-2-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features redis-session --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` with exit 0 after fetching and compiling the git-pinned FrankenRedis crates.
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.

Residual note:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` is classified by `rch` as non-compilation and did not provide a meaningful remote validation artifact, so I did not count it as evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3.1][support] Replace Frankentui sibling path deps with pinned git deps for deterministic rch workers","description":"Bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3.1. Replace the optional Frankentui subcrate dependencies in crates/frankenterm-core from sibling path references (../../../frankentui/...) to pinned git dependencies so remote rch workers do not require an out-of-repo frankentui checkout. Preserve local development convenience via config.toml.example override guidance, update Cargo.lock, and validate a remote feature path that resolves the ftui subcrates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:48.393096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04.884693Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04.884638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:07:48.393096Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2126,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the bounded Frankentui manifest slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml`: replaced the optional Frankentui subcrate sibling path deps (`../../../frankentui/...`) with pinned git deps against `https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui` at `rev 18facd3f2a2c51b8b06ff589e6b905e6a63f5a96`.
- `.cargo/config.toml.example`: extended the existing Frankentui patch block so local sibling-checkout development can still intentionally override `ftui`, `ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, and `ftui-backend`.

Why:
- ProudMeadow's topology preflight artifact showed the current first missing remote manifest was `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankentui/crates/ftui-backend/Cargo.toml`, indicating a second ambient sibling-layout assumption after the FrankenRedis fix.

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- crates/frankenterm-core/Cargo.toml .cargo/config.toml.example Cargo.lock` -> clean.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/brightoak-rch-ft-3681t-7-3-1-3-check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --features tui-dashboard --message-format short` -> remote execution on `vmi1152480` reached and resolved the Frankentui git crates successfully (`ftui-core`, `ftui-render`, `ftui-layout`, `ftui-style`, `ftui-widgets`, `ftui-runtime`, `ftui-backend` all appeared as git-sourced in the remote check output).
- That remote check then failed with unrelated pre-existing `policy_diagnostics.rs` compile errors (`KillSwitch.level`, missing telemetry fields, missing `snapshot` methods) rather than any missing Frankentui path-manifest error.

Residual note:
- I did not modify `Cargo.lock` for this slice because the workspace was already carrying the same pinned Frankentui git revision; the lockfile still contains duplicate `ftui-*` entries, but the active manifest path used by the remote worker resolved through the git source rather than a sibling checkout.
- Treat the remaining `tui-dashboard` validation failure as blocked on the existing `policy_diagnostics.rs` compile break, not on remote worker topology for Frankentui.","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:14:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3][support] Enforce rch for heavy cargo SendText policy path","description":"Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3: add reusable heavy-cargo/rch classification helpers in build_coord and enforce require-approval for non-human SendText attempts that invoke heavy cargo commands without an rch wrapper. Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFox","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33.245598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2036,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Implemented the bounded policy/build_coord slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.
","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-3681t.7.4","title":"[ops] Chaos + scale validation harness (1k-pane/connector stress)","description":"Build a rigorous resilience harness covering large pane counts, bursty connector traffic, policy churn, and partial outages to validate SLOs before migration cutover.","acceptance_criteria":"1.
